title: Add reproducible demo runbook and browser UI E2E proof
goal: Close the post-deliverability demo evidence gap with a repeatable runbook and browser-level UI E2E covering resource sync, deployment/task-pull status, logs, and monitoring.
forbidden_scope: No live deploy or rollback unless an isolated local fake target is verified.
No broad verification output in the main context.
acceptance: Runbook covers local DB/Redis, agent target, project onboarding, deployment dry-run, task-pull completed, logs, monitoring, and rollback checkpoints.
Browser-level Playwright E2E is committed as a reproducible script and covers resource sync, execution governance/task-pull status, logs, and monitoring pages.
Noisy command output is stored under /tmp/codex-tool-runs/svton/.
Live deploy-completed-rollback is executed against a safe fake target or recorded as a concrete blocker.
